task_name: Builder migration wave 3: provider, gate, and write path
goal: Migrate the network/provider/write-path Builder layer into bluepilot/builder/src: 9 machine-verified modules covering provider calls, maya-core gate checks, canonical push/write helpers, and error learning against the migrated DB. Tests must pass without live API keys, without a live DB, and without a reachable maya-core.
scope_out: Do not migrate orchestrator, pipeline, judge, architect, or route/controller modules.
Do not change module logic; only relocate imports required by the new builder/src layout.
Do not require live API keys, a live DB, or reachable maya-core for tests.
Do not modify Bluepilot root package.json or tools/*.cjs.
claims: The 9-module manifest is the recursive closure of the provider/write-path seeds, excluding already migrated wave 1 and 2 modules.
mayaBuilderGateClient exists in soulmatch and is required by providers and opusSmartPush.
The closure pulls no top module: no opusTaskOrchestrator, opusBuildPipeline, architectPhase1, opusJudge, opusRenderBridge, opusSelfTest, opusFeatureOrchestrator, opusVordenker, or opusMeisterPlan.
outboundHttp requires undici, so builder/package.json and package-lock.json must be updated.
opusErrorLearning needs only import relocation for db and schema paths.
The original mayaBuilderGateClient.test.ts is not migrated verbatim because it imports builderGithubBridge; BP-129 adds a focused gate-client test instead.
assumptions: Provider/API-key behavior should remain environment based and not be exercised live in BP-129 tests.
The maya-core gate should fail closed for premium model calls and irreversible write checks when unavailable.
Live provider keys and maya-core deployment are operator infrastructure concerns documented for later runs.
dod: All 9 manifest modules exist under builder/src.
All migrated modules match soulmatch sources except documented import-path relocations.
opusSmartPush.test.ts is migrated with test imports relocated to ../src.
builder/tests/mayaBuilderGateClient.test.ts validates focused fail-closed and gate-header behavior without builderGithubBridge or live maya-core.
builder/.env.example documents provider keys, MAYA_CORE_URL, and gate token variables.
npm test in builder/ passes without live API keys, live DB, or reachable maya-core.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
review-packets/BP-129.md contains provenance, closure proof, relocation list, evidence, and infra notes.
stop_conditions: The 9-module closure pulls a top module.
A live provider key, live DB, or reachable maya-core becomes necessary for tests.
A root package or WLP tool change becomes necessary.
soulmatch would need to be changed.
prior_task_findings: BP-128 added the Bluepilot Builder DB foundation. A pre-build closure check corrected BP-129 from 8 to 9 modules because mayaBuilderGateClient exists and is imported by providers and opusSmartPush.
